equalizer.title: Equalizer
equalizer.tooltip: Shapes which frequencies drive the visuals. Does not change audio output. Boost bass for more intense movement, cut highs to calm treble reactions
equalizer.preAmpTooltip: Scales the overall signal. Higher makes visuals more reactive, lower calms them. Purely visual, does not affect audio output

rendering.title: Rendering
rendering.frameRateTooltip: Lower frame rates reduce GPU usage
rendering.resolutionTooltip: Lower resolution reduces GPU load but looks less sharp
rendering.meshResolutionTooltip: Controls the vertex grid for warp distortions. Higher = finer detail at more GPU cost
rendering.textureQualityTooltip: Internal render resolution multiplier. Lower = faster, higher = supersampled quality
rendering.antiAliasingTooltip: Smooth jagged edges with FXAA. Small performance cost (~2-3%)
rendering.brightnessTooltip: Dims the visualizer. Useful in dark rooms or when you want subtle ambient visuals
rendering.audioSmoothingTooltip: Lower = snappier reaction to beats, higher = smoother movement. Effect is subtle: presets apply their own internal smoothing
rendering.fftSizeTooltip: Higher = more frequency detail but more latency. Effect is subtle: the engine maps to fixed internal frequency bands

presets.title: Presets
presets.tooltip: Presets vary in how reactive they are to sound. If visuals feel too subtle or too intense, adjust Pre-Amp gain in the Equalizer tab
presets.transitionTimeTooltip: How long presets blend into each other when switching
presets.presetNameDisplayTooltip: How long the preset name shows when switching presets
presets.autopilotTooltip: Automatically cycles through presets at the set interval. Uses shuffle-style rounds: every preset plays once before any repeats
presets.modeTooltip: All = presets from selected packs, Favorites = only favorited presets (ignores pack selection)
presets.favoriteFrequencyTooltip: How much more likely favorites are to be picked next. Every preset still plays once per round before any repeats. This just makes favorites come up sooner

data.title: Data
data.description: Your settings are stored locally in this browser on this device. Use export and import to transfer settings between browsers or devices, or to back them up.
data.descriptionWithSync: Your settings are stored locally in this browser on this device. If connected via Spotify, they also sync to the cloud. Use export and import to transfer settings between browsers or devices, or to back them up.
data.importDescription: Select categories to import. Only selected categories will be overwritten. Everything else stays as-is.
data.loadFileDescription: Load a settings file to choose which categories to apply.
data.includeImportedHint: Embeds raw preset and texture data for cross-device transfer

spotifyTab.title: Spotify
spotifyTab.unavailable: Spotify integration is not available. If you are self-hosting MangoWave, see the README for setup instructions.
spotifyTab.connectedInfo: Now-playing metadata & cloud-synced MangoWave settings active. Playback controls require Premium. Share a screen, window, or tab playing Spotify so the visualizer can hear audio.
spotifyTab.connectDescription: Connect Spotify for now-playing metadata and cloud-synced MangoWave settings. Spotify Premium also enables playback controls. You'll still need to share a screen, window, or tab playing Spotify for the visualizer to react to audio.
spotifyTab.devInfo: If you are not the app owner, ensure they've added your name and email associated with your Spotify account to their developer app's User Management tab.

sync.title: Sync
sync.windowSyncDescription: Sync presets and settings across multiple browser windows on the same device. Open MangoWave in multiple windows to visualize on multiple monitors. Works best with system audio or microphone — each window captures the same source. Local file playback is per-window.
sync.unavailable: Multi-window sync is not supported in this browser.
sync.autoSyncNote: Presets, EQ, autopilot, favorites, and all other settings automatically sync across windows. Change something on one and the others will update. Turn Window Sync on or off in each window separately.
sync.syncPerformanceHint: Frame rate, resolution, mesh, texture quality, and anti-aliasing. Uncheck if your monitors need different performance settings.

deviceSync.title: Device Sync
deviceSync.description: Sync presets across different devices (phone, laptop, TV) via peer-to-peer WebRTC. One device creates a room, others join with the room code. The room creator controls autopilot.
deviceSync.hostDisconnected: The room host disconnected. The room has been closed.
deviceSync.invalidCode: Invalid room code. Format: MANGO-XXXX (4 letters, no I or O)
deviceSync.roomNotFound: Room not found. Check the code and try again.
deviceSync.syncSettingsHint: Share EQ, pre-amp, audio analysis, and rendering settings across devices. Uncheck if devices need different performance settings.
deviceSync.autopilotNote: Autopilot settings always sync — any device can toggle autopilot, but only the room creator's timer fires.

language.title: Language
language.tooltip: Choose the display language for the interface
